## Purpose
This file serves to specify which files and directories should be ignored by version control systems and the DiffDoc tool, preventing them from being tracked or processed.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will observe that the specified files and directories are excluded from version control operations, such as commits and diffs. This helps maintain a cleaner repository by omitting unnecessary files.

## Business Rules
The file defines rules for ignoring various types of files, including version control artifacts, dependency caches, build outputs, logs, environment files, and binary assets. These rules ensure that only relevant files are included in version control.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
No data inputs are processed by this file. The output is a set of ignored file patterns that dictate which files should not be tracked by version control.

## Side Effects
The primary side effect is the exclusion of specified files from version control operations, which can lead to a more streamlined repository and reduced clutter.

## Error And Edge Cases
None identified.

## Dependencies
None identified.

## Operational Notes
This file should be maintained to reflect any changes in the project's structure or dependencies. It is crucial for ensuring that unnecessary files do not interfere with version control processes.
